title: Volume of a cylinder
problem: A cylindrical water tank has a radius of $2$ m and a height of $5$ m. Find the volume of the tank. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.

steps.instruction: Write the formula for the volume of a cylinder.

title: Volume of a cone and converting to capacity
problem: A conical container has a radius of $6$ cm and a perpendicular height of $14$ cm. Find the volume of the container, then state the capacity in millilitres. Give the volume to 2 decimal places.

steps.instruction: Write the formula for the volume of a cone.
Convert to millilitres using $1$ cm$^3$ $= 1$ mL.

title: Volume using the trapezoidal rule
problem: A section of a river has an irregular cross-section. The width at the front is $8$ m and the width at the rear is $12$ m, with a perpendicular distance of $3$ m between the two measurements. The section of river being measured is $25$ m long. Use the trapezoidal rule $A \approx \dfrac{h}{2}(d_f + d_l)$ to estimate the volume of water in this section.

steps.instruction: Identify the values for the trapezoidal rule: $d_f = 8$ m, $d_l = 12$ m, $h = 3$ m.
Apply the trapezoidal rule to estimate the cross-sectional area.
Multiply the cross-sectional area by the length of the section to find the volume.
